Pass the value of z to a non-linear activation function. 

Activation functions are used to introduce non-linearity into the output of the neurons, without which the neural network will just be a linear function. Moreover, they have a significant impact on the learning speed of the neural network. 


Perceptrons have binary step function as their activation function. However, we shall use sigmoid also known as logistic function as our activation function.


Apply a non-linear activation function on the value of z. The activation function of perceptrons is a binary step function. However, we will utilise the sigmoid function, commonly known as the logistic function, as our activation function.


Backpropagation, short for backward propagation of errors, refers to the algorithm for computing the gradient of the loss function with respect to the weights.

Backpropagation, also known as backward error propagation, is the technique for determining the gradient of the loss function with respect to the weights.


To know an estimation of how far are we from our desired solution a loss function is used. Generally, mean squared error is chosen as the loss function for regression problems and cross entropy for classification problems.

A loss function is used to estimate how far we are from our intended answer. For regression problems, mean squared error is typically used as the loss function, while cross entropy is used for classification problems.


The Loss function is computed for the whole training dataset, and the mean is referred to as the Cost function C.

The model try to optimize the value of the weight and bias which brings the cost function as low as possible by finding its vertex as global minimum on a convex graph or a non-convex graph.




In order to find the best weights and bias for our Perceptron, we need to know how the cost function changes in relation to weights and bias. This is done with the help of the gradients (rate of change) — how one quantity changes in relation to another quantity. In our case, we need to find the gradient of the cost function with respect to the weights and bias.
Let’s calculate the gradient of cost function C with respect to the weight wᵢ using partial derivation. Since the cost function is not directly related to the weight wᵢ, let’s use the chain rule.

To determine the optimal weights and bias for the Perceptron, first understand the cost function varies with weights and bias. The cost function with regard to the weights and bias is identified as an optimal solution using gradients (rate of change).
Using partial derivation, compute the gradient of the cost function C with respect to the weight wi. Use the chain rule since the cost function is not directly connected to the weight wi.


the gradient of the cost function (C) with respect to the predicted value ( ŷ )

the gradient of the predicted value with respect to the z.

The gradient of z with respect to the weight wᵢ

Optimization is the selection of the best element from some set of available alternatives. The weights and bias are updated as follows and the backpropagation and gradient descent is repeated until convergence. 

The selection of the best element from a group of accessible options is known as optimization. Backpropagation and gradient descent are continued until completion, and the weights and bias are updated as follows.



Nature has been an inspiration for the introduction of many metaheuristic algorithms. Different animals communicate with each other through different mode of communications. Fireflies use their flashing property to communicate. The flashing communication is used to attract their mate and also to warn predators. Based on the pattern of the light, a suitable mate will communicate back by either mimicking the same pattern or responding with a specific pattern. It also needs to be noted that the light intensity decreases through distance; hence, a flashing light emanating from a firefly gets a response from fireflies around it within a visual range of the flash.

Firefly algorithm is a swarm-based metaheuristic algorithm which was introduced by Yang [5]. The algorithm mimics how fireflies interact using their flashing lights. The algorithm assumes that all fireflies are unisex, which means any firefly can be attracted by any other firefly; the attractiveness of a firefly is directly proportional to its brightness which depends on the objective function. A firefly will be attracted to a brighter firefly. Furthermore, the brightness decreases through distance based on inverse square law, as given in Eq. (2).

If the light is passing through a medium with a light absorption coefficient γ, then the light intensity at a distance of r from the source can be given as in Eq. (3).


where I0 is light intensity at the source. Similarly, the brightness, β, can be given as in Eq. (4).

A generalized brightness function for ω ≥ 1 is given in Eq. (5) [5]. In fact, any monotonically decreasing function can be used.

Install the Heroku CLI
Download and install the Heroku CLI.

If you haven't already, log in to your Heroku account and follow the prompts to create a new SSH public key.

$ heroku login
Create a new Git repository
Initialize a git repository in a new or existing directory

$ cd my-project/
$ git init
$ heroku git:remote -a farming-12
Deploy your application
Commit your code to the repository and deploy it to Heroku using Git.

$ git add .
$ git commit -am "make it better"
$ git push heroku master
You can now change your main deploy branch from "master" to "main" for both manual and automatic deploys, please follow the instructions here.
Existing Git repository
For existing repositories, simply add the heroku remote

$ heroku git:remote -a farming-12

xzi = (5,6)   ---> *

xi + (ED(xzi-xi)) * ld

ld == rnd(0 to 1)  ---> *

(2,4) + ( |5-2| , |6-4| ) * 0.19

Existing oversampling methods have issues with outliers and class overlapping among existing variations of SMOTE.  A novel modified synthetic minority oversampling method is presented called M-SMOTE to address this problem. Proposed MSMOTE performs the oversampling for minority classes without class overlapping. M-SMOTE receives two input parameters like Dataset and Dy( Name of the dependent variable). The first step of the M-SMOTE technique is outlier detection and mitigation. 
M-SMOTE employs the RemoveOutlier function, which detects and eliminates outliers using the Z-score method. Equation 1 uses equations 2 and 3 to calculate the Z-score. Equations 2 and 3 compute mean and standard deviation, respectively.  The upper and lower bounds are obtained by employing equations 4 and 5, respectively. Equation 6 finds the outliers. Equation 7 removes the outliers to create a noise/outlier-free dataset. The dataset is divided into the dependent(Dy) and independent(Dx) variables using equation 8, and finally, a dataset containing Dx and Dy are returned to the M-SMOTE function.   
The length of the majority class is calculated using equation 9, where CMJ  represents the name of majority class. The list of the minority class is computed using equation 10 and is list is assigned CMN. Total minority classes (M)  are obtained using equation 11. The ratio of minority class with respect to majority class is computed using equation 12. If the ratio obtained is less than 0.8 then the class is declared as imbalanced class and  using equation 13, the total number of synthetic samples(G) to be generated for minority classes is calculated. Using the equation 14, G samples are generated and added to the dataset. 
This method generates a new dataset with no outlier values and thus, provides the noise-free dataset for generating the synthetic samples for minority classes.





Selection of instances from minority class, sample attribute, sample types, sampling rate, the strategy of samples distribution from minority class is a  complex optimization problem. These problems need mathematical modeling to find the optimal solution. The DCS and  PNM algorithm is an optimization algorithm that uses randomized search techniques to find the solution to a multi-dimensional nonlinear complex problem [22]. The DCS and PNM algorithm is commonly used in operation and engineering optimization problems[23].  Hence, the proposed method uses the smote technique using DCS and PNM along with the KNN algorithm for imbalanced dataset handling.

To generate new samples for minority classes, SMOTE does not examine adjacent samples but uses the samples from the different classes, leading to more class overlap and noise. SMOTE does not handle the high dimensional data and generates the duplicates samples for minority classes. Thus, after applying SMOTE, the dataset can contain many duplicates samples which affect the model accuracy. These constitute a significant limitation of SMOTE method. The proposed method hybridizes the SMOTE, progressive neighborhood method(PNM), duplicate count strategy(DCS)++ algorithm, and k-nearest neighbors(KNN) algorithm to overcome the limitation of SMOTE and hence the proposed hybridized method is called  H-SMOTE. After feature selection, the proposed system uses the H-SMOTE method to generate the synthetic samples for minority classes to make the unique and uniform number of samples for each attribute. 
Often, SMOTE generates duplicates and noisy samples in the dataset and hence such dataset affects the accuracy of the model. Proposes system applies the DCS++ and progressive neighborhood method(PNM) to obtain the duplicate samples of the dataset. PNM analyzes samples that are inside a window of samples that are previously divided in sorted order. DCS++  method is the most efficient method to search duplicates samples in the dataset. The DCS++ algorithm sorts the dataset is by a key, and then a window is moved over all of the entries to find all of the duplicates. After that, the proposed system finds K- unique neighboring samples for each duplicate sample by considering the boundary or range of each attribute. Then, the mean of these k-unique neighboring samples is computed to replace the duplicate sample. This process is carried out repeatedly until the dataset contains the unique samples within the permissible range of the attribute.

TensorFlow-based Automatic Personality Recognition Used in Asynchronous Video Interviews
ABSTRACT With the development of artificial intelligence (AI), the automatic analysis of video interviews to recognize individual personality traits has become an active area of research and has applications in personality computing, human-computer interaction, and psychological assessment. Advances in computer vision and pattern recognition based on deep learning (DL) techniques have led to the establishment of convolutional neural network (CNN) models that can successfully recognize human nonverbal cues and attribute their personality traits with the use of a camera. In this study, an end-to-end AI interviewing system was developed using asynchronous video interview (AVI) processing and a TensorFlow AI engine to perform automatic personality recognition (APR) based on the features extracted from the AVIs and the true personality scores from the facial expressions and self-reported questionnaires of 120 real job applicants. The experimental results show that our AI-based interview agent can successfully recognize the "big five" traits of an interviewee at an accuracy between 90.9% and 97.4%. Our experiment also indicates that although the machine learning was conducted without large-scale data, the semisupervised DL approach performed surprisingly well with regard to automatic personality recognition despite the lack of labor-intensive manual annotation and labeling. The AI-based interview agent can supplement or replace existing self-reported personality assessment methods that job applicants may distort to achieve socially desirable effects.

#print(soup.find_all('p'))
 for tag in p_tags:
    listofalltext.append(tag.text)
   #print (tag.text)
 for tag in abbr_tags:
    listofalldate.append(tag.text)
 for i in range(len(listofalltext)):
   talldata.append (listofalltext[1])
    fbpostdata-fbpostdatatlistofalltext[i]+'
    alldata.append(listofalltext[i]+"@-ilistofalldate[i])
 #print(listofall(ij+"@"+listofalidate[i])
 print(alldata)
 return alldata






LEAD GENERATION FOR SALES:
Scraped data from the internet based on particular keywords and city, and extracted useful information like company name, rating, category or domain, and contact information like phone number and website of the the company, which the sales department uses to offer the products and services to other companies on B2B level and to clients.
To reach out and offer our products and services to other companies on B2B level and to clients.
Python 3.8(BeautifulSoup, Selenium), MS Excel
ALGORITHMIC TRADING:
- GETTING DATA(CCXT)
- STORING THE DATA
- VISUALIZING THE DATA
- ANALYSING THE DATA (TIMESERIES)
- TRADE EXECUTION (CCXT) (BOT)
- TRADE ANALYSIS
- BACKTESTING
- RISK MANAGEMENT





# Lib's

import RPi.GPIO as GPIO
import time

GPIO.setmode(GPIO.BCM)
GPIO.cleanup()
GPIO.setwarnings(False)
 
# GPIO Pin's
GPIO.setup(23,GPIO.OUT)
GPIO.setup(24,GPIO.OUT)

p = GPIO.PWM(23,40)
q = GPIO.PWM(24,50)

p.start(0)
q.start(0)
print ("starting 0")
p.ChangeDutyCycle(3)
q.ChangeDutyCycle(3)
print("start")
time.sleep(2)

i = 4.5

while True:
    # p.ChangeDutyCycle(9)
    # time.sleep(1)
    # i = 3
    # while i<9:
    #     print(i)

    # Forword 
    while i<5.9:
        print('Fast : ',round(i,1))
        # p.ChangeDutyCycle(i), q.ChangeDutyCycle(i) 
        p.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
        time.sleep(0.6)
        q.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        i +=.2

    while i>4.4:
        print('Slow : ',round(i,1))
        # p.ChangeDutyCycle(i), q.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
        p.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        q.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        i -=.2
    
    # left
    while i<5.9:
        print('Left Fast : ',round(i,1))
        p.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        q.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        i +=.2

    while i>4.4:
        print('Left Slow : ',round(i,1))
        p.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        q.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        i -=.2
    
    # right
    while i<5.9:
        print('Right Fast : ',round(i,1))
        p.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        q.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
        time.sleep(0.2)
        i +=.2

    while i>4.4:
        print('Right Slow : ',round(i,1))

        time.sleep(0.2)

        time.sleep(0.2)

import numpy as np
import cv2, datetime
from pyrebase import pyrebase
import RPi.GPIO as GPIO
import time
GPIO.setmode(GPIO.BCM)
GPIO.cleanup()
GPIO.setwarnings(False)

# GPIO Pin's
# M1
GPIO.setup(3,GPIO.OUT)
GPIO.setup(4,GPIO.OUT)
# M2
GPIO.setup(17,GPIO.OUT)
GPIO.setup(27,GPIO.OUT)

# PM1 & PM2
GPIO.setup(23,GPIO.OUT)
GPIO.setup(24,GPIO.OUT)

p = GPIO.PWM(23,40)
q = GPIO.PWM(24,50)

p.start(0)
q.start(0)
print ("starting 0")
p.ChangeDutyCycle(3)
q.ChangeDutyCycle(3)
print("start")
time.sleep(0.5)

i = 4.5

#Initialize Firebase
firebaseConfig = {
    "apiKey": "AIzaSyCC9yQmLunYTTqk77gIID43-hXN9uwHJqw",
    "authDomain": "projectboat-5a97e.firebaseapp.com",
    "databaseURL": "https://projectboat-5a97e-default-rtdb.firebaseio.com",
    "projectId": "projectboat-5a97e",
    "storageBucket": "projectboat-5a97e.appspot.com",
    "messagingSenderId": "418938233811",
    "appId": "1:418938233811:web:5608bbcffa2edaa247c526",
    "measurementId": "G-6K6J2LSLTV"
  }
firebase=pyrebase.initialize_app(firebaseConfig)
db=firebase.database()

cap = cv2.VideoCapture(0)
x = datetime.datetime.now()
fileName = str(x.strftime("%d")+x.strftime("%m")+x.strftime("%Y")+x.strftime("%H")+x.strftime("%M")+x.strftime("%S"))

# Define the codec and create VideoWriter object
fourcc = cv2.VideoWriter_fourcc(*'mp4v')
out = cv2.VideoWriter(fileName+'.avi', fourcc, 5, (640,480))

def forword():
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.HIGH)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.HIGH)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.LOW)
    time.sleep(0.3)

def back():
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.HIGH)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.HIGH)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.LOW)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.LOW)
    time.sleep(0.3)

def left():
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.HIGH)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.HIGH)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.LOW)
    time.sleep(0.3)

def right():
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.HIGH)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.HIGH)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.LOW)
    time.sleep(0.2)
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.LOW)
    time.sleep(0.3)

def stop():
    GPIO.output(3,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(4,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(17,GPIO.LOW)
    GPIO.output(27,GPIO.LOW)
    time.sleep(0.2)

while (cap.isOpened()):
    ret, frame = cap.read()
    cv2.imshow('frame',frame)
    out.write(frame)
    
    mode = db.child("mode").get()
    on = db.child("on").get()
    direction = db.child("direction").get()
    print(mode.val(),' -> ',on.val(),' -> ',direction.val()) 

    if mode.val() == 'manual' and on.val() == 'land':
        if direction.val() == 'forward':
            # forword
            forword()
        
        elif direction.val() == 'back':
            # back
            back()

        elif direction.val() == 'left':
            # left
            left()

        elif direction.val() == 'right':
            # right
            right()

        else:
            # Stop
            stop()

    if mode.val() == 'manual' and on.val() == 'water':
        if direction.val() == 'forward':
            # forword
            while i<6.9:
                p.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                q.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                i +=.2
            while i>4.8:
                p.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                q.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                i -=1.2
        
        elif direction.val() == 'left':
            # left
            while i<6.9:
                p.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                q.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                i +=.2
            while i>4.8:
                p.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                q.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                i -=1.2

        elif direction.val() == 'right':
            # right
            while i<6.9:
                p.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                q.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                i +=.2
            while i>4.4:
                p.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                q.ChangeDutyCycle(i)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                i -=1.2

        else:
            # Stop
                p.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
                time.sleep(0.1)
                q.ChangeDutyCycle(0)
                time.sleep(0.1)

    if cv2.waitKey(1) & 0xFF == ord('q'):
        break

# Release everything if job is finished
cap.release()
cv2.destroyAllWindows()

The authors should consider the following suggestions provided by the reviewer in order to improve the scientific depth of their manuscript, as well as they should address the following comments in order to improve the quality of presentation of their manuscript.

Initially, in Section 1, the authors should provide an introductory discussion on the basic machine learning approaches that have been used in the literature to perform the data classification, such as Huang, Xin-Lin, Xiaomin Ma, and Fei Hu. "Machine learning and intelligent communications." Mobile Networks and Applications 23.1 (2018): 68-70, which can be used in several research fields of data collected by Internet of Things sensors.

In Section 2, the authors should discuss several other similar application approaches, such as Tsiropoulou, E. E., et al. "RFID-based smart parking management system." Cyber-Physical Systems 3.1-4 (2017): 22-41, where the proposed methodology can be applied other than for the water quality management.

In Section 3, the authors should discuss the structure of the collected data from the Internet of Things sensors and the process that has been followed in order to collect them from the field.

The presentation of figure 2 needs to be improved as currently it is quite blurry.

In section 3, the authors should include an additional subsection providing the theoretical analysis of the computational complexity of the proposed framework as well as the corresponding implementation cost.

In Section 4, the authors should complement the answer to the previous comment by introducing and presenting some numerical results quantifying the computational complexity of the proposed framework by showing the real execution time in order the data to be processed.

Finally, the overall manuscript should be checked for typos, syntax, and grammar errors in order to improve the quality of its presentation.


Reviewer #3: General comments
=============
Thank you for the opportunity to review this paper. Overall, it's a very interesting work to design an IoT based prediction system for classification of water. Nonetheless, several issues should be noted:

Specific comments
=============
Major comments
---------------------

1. Major English language editing should be considered to improve readability.
2. In abstract, the authors stated "Machine learning models give poor accuracy on unseen data, and therefore, the testing accuracy of the trained model is affected". Please justify your statement.
3. The authors didn't give details about the data (e.g., # of dimensions, splits of training/test/validation) and the modeling framework (e.g., tensorflow, pytorch or other?).
4. A major concern is that it seems the authors applied G-SMOTE on all of the data. Data augmentation on validation and test data will distort the true distribution and will very likely to overrate the model's performance. G-SMOTE should be applied on the training data only. The model should be tested and validated on true distribution.
5. The authors may consider removing well-known contents from the methods part, such as the definition of performance metrics.
6. In "Client-side Deployment" under "Model Deployment", the authors mentioned "Model drift calculates the similarity between the real-time input and the records presents in the dataset" but didn't give the similarity metric.

I think instead of dropping "either of" 2 highly correlated features, we should check from both of them how each of them correlates with the target as well and then drop the less correlated with the target variable. Which might increase some accuracy instead of considering dropping whichever comes first. Again, I think it is.


If you are transporting ice-cream in a vehicle, the number of ice-cream sticks that reach the destination is inversely proportional to temperature, higher the temperature, lesser are the sticks.
If you want to effectively model the temperature of the vehicle's cooler and make it optimal, you need to consider this negatively correlated features, outside air temperature and number of ice-cream sticks at the destination.

Refactoring Faces under Bounding Box using Instance Segmentation Algorithms in Deep Learning for replacement of Editing Tools
Sep 2020 – Apr 2021

Project descriptionImmense amount of funds and time are being invested in video and photo editing. This has an involvement of resources in a high number. Software as well as hardware. Software is handled by an entire editing team. Hardware is used for the purpose of supporting the editing tools. Resource exhaustion is the main cause and is detrimental for the reach of the organization. Deep Learning is being used as a replacement for a lot of heavy ended monotonous work. Under this notion, the same can be extended to this specific problem. Automating the problem using Neural Network intended for Image Processing applications. This network will directly do all the necessary amount of work and refactor the entire image and video without using any specific tool or special effect.

y_pred2, le.inverse_transform(y_pred2)[0]

pickle.dump(dt_Classifier, open(filename, 'wb'))

y_pred2 = rf_Classifier.predict(np.array([input_list]))
print(y_pred2, le.inverse_transform(y_pred2)[0])

# save the model to disk
filename = 'rf_Classifier.pkl'
pickle.dump(rf_Classifier, open(filename, 'wb'))

    * 'lr' - Logistic Regression             
    * 'knn' - K Neighbors Classifier          
    * 'nb' - Naive Bayes             
    * 'dt' - Decision Tree Classifier                   
    * 'svm' - SVM - Linear Kernel               
    * 'rbfsvm' - SVM - Radial Kernel               
    * 'gpc' - Gaussian Process Classifier                  
    * 'mlp' - MLP Classifier                  
    * 'ridge' - Ridge Classifier                
    * 'rf' - Random Forest Classifier                   
    * 'qda' - Quadratic Discriminant Analysis                  
    * 'ada' - Ada Boost Classifier                 
    * 'gbc' - Gradient Boosting Classifier                  
    * 'lda' - Linear Discriminant Analysis                  
    * 'et' - Extra Trees Classifier                   
    * 'xgboost' - Extreme Gradient Boosting              
    * 'lightgbm' - Light Gradient Boosting Machine             
    * 'catboost' - CatBoost Classifier

Objective: 

Problem 

1.AICTE grants approval for establishment of new technical institutions and starting of new courses in the field of Engineering and Technology, Management, Pharmacy, Architecture etc. At present the criteria for grant of approval for such courses is based mainly on infrastructure available as per the minimum norms prescribed by AICTE (Approval Process Handbook). However due to lack of information on employment potential for the proposed courses AICTE is giving approval and the students pass out from such Institutions/Branches of Engineering/Management/Pharmacy etc remain unemployed.

At present, there is no mechanism available to find out how to estimate/forecast employment potential for any branch(s)/course(s) in Engineering/Management/Pharmacy etc on short term and long term basis by correlating data from various sources and formulating computer program/application using neural networks OR any other programming tools.

Solution expected:

 Development of Application Software to estimate/forecast employment potential of Graduates/Post Graduates in different branches/courses on short term and long term basis by correlating data from various sources could be useful for granting approval along with infrastructure and other facilities. 

Modern era AI and computing systems can be employed to help in the process and any significant innovation in this field is highly appreciated which considers multiple factors and forecasts the admissions and jobs sector scenarios. 

There is a need to forecast the current trend in the admissions and job sectors so as to blend the courses and syllabus accordingly to keep the youth employed and skilled with rapidly changing world."


Pls look on these problem statement. Pls suggest implementation strategies

import math
class Circle:
    def __init__(self,radius):
        self.radius=radius
    def area(self):
        print("Area is",math.pi*math.pow(self.radius,2))






Write a program to accept 2 lists from the user of 5 nos each . Assume each list will have unique nos Now find out how many items in these lists are common .

Write a program to accept 5 unique integers from the user. Make sure if the integer being entered is already present in the list your code displays the message 
“Item already present” and ask the user to reenter the integer. 





i=1
sortednums=[]
print("Enter any 5 random integers:")
while i<=5:
	n=int(input())
	pos=0
	for x in sortednums:
		if x>n:
		   break
		pos=pos+1   	
	sortednums.insert(pos,n)
	i=i+1
print("Sorted list is:")
print(sortednums)


-----------------------------------------

i =0 
l = []
while i < 5:
    x = int(input("Enter a number"))
    l.append(x)
    i+=1


for i in range(len(l)):
    for j in range(len(l)):
        if (l[i] < l[j]):
            l[j], l[i] = l[i], l[j]

print(l)

# WAFun to fetch data from SQL table via API.
# WAFun to fetch data from mongodb table via API.
'''
# for api (flask)
from flask import Flask, request, jsonify
# for database
import sqlite3
import pymongo
# get current date and time
import datetime

def insert_into_sqlite(sql_connect, sql_cursor):
    '''
    The function is to insert a data into sqlite database.
    '''
    data = str(datetime.datetime.now())
    sql_cursor.execute(f'CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS data_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, data TEXT)')
    sql_cursor.execute(f'INSERT INTO data_table (data) VALUES ("{data}")')
    sql_connect.commit()

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/api/fetch_data_from_mongo', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def fetch_data_from_mongo():
    if (request.method == 'POST'):
        mongo_client = pymongo.MongoClient("mongodb+srv://mongo:mongo@cluster0.4xfpx.mongodb.net/myFirstDatabase?retryWrites=true&w=majority")
        mongo_db = mongo_client.test1
        mongo_collection = mongo_db['my_collection']
        mongo_document = mongo_collection.find() #.limit(10)
        data = [i for i in mongo_document]
        return jsonify(str(data))

@app.route('/api/fetch_data_from_sqlite', methods=['GET', 'POST'])
def fetch_data_from_sqlite():
    if (request.method == 'POST'):
        sql_connect = sqlite3.connect('test_db.db')
        sql_cursor = sql_connect.cursor()
        insert_into_sqlite(sql_connect, sql_cursor)
        sql_cursor.execute(f'SELECT * FROM data_table')
        table_data = sql_cursor.fetchall()
        return jsonify(str(table_data))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True)

from flask import Flask, render_template, request, jsonify

app = Flask(__name__)


@app.route('/', methods=['GET', 'POST']) # To render Homepage
def home_page():
    return render_template('index.html')
	
@app.route('/math', methods=['POST'])  # This will be called from UI
def math_operation():
    if (request.method=='POST'):
        operation=request.form['operation']
        num1=int(request.form['num1'])
        num2 = int(request.form['num2'])
        if(operation=='add'):
            r=num1+num2
            result= 'the sum of '+str(num1)+' and '+str(num2) +' is '+str(r)
        if (operation == 'subtract'):
            r = num1 - num2
            result = 'the difference of ' + str(num1) + ' and ' + str(num2) + ' is ' + str(r)
        if (operation == 'multiply'):
            r = num1 * num2
            result = 'the product of ' + str(num1) + ' and ' + str(num2) + ' is ' + str(r)
        if (operation == 'divide'):
            r = num1 / num2
            result = 'the quotient when ' + str(num1) + ' is divided by ' + str(num2) + ' is ' + str(r)
        return render_template('results.html',result=result)

def math_operation_via_postman():
    if (request.method=='POST'):3VUFZ68L52E3KFARRYL01MPhttps://youtu.be/jpc-DfhFMtY

        num1=int(request.json['num1'])
        num2 = int(request.json['num2'])
        if(operation=='add'):
            r=num
            result= 'the sum of '+str(num1)+' and '+str(num2) +' is '+str(r)tgv6fvbghgvb

        if (operation == 'divide'):
            r = num1 / num2
            result = 'the quotient when ' + str(num1) + ' is divided by ' + str(num2) + ' is ' + str(r)
        return jsonify(result)

    app.run(debug=True)

================================================================================================================



An iot are beneficial for the different sectors of industry like Information Technology, Agriculture, Health Care, Energy, Transport, and so on. IoT uses robots, drones, remote sensors and computer imaging combined with continuously progressing Artificial Intelligence (AI) and analytical tools for controlling, monitoring, surveying, GPS geotagging and mapping related complex task with the good quality, accuracy and precision. An industry related to environment like Agriculture and Energy are produce thousands of data points on temperature, soil, usage of water, weather condition, air quality etc. This data is leveraged in real-time for obtaining useful insights and taking the right decision at the right time also save both time and money. The applications of IoT are target conventional operations to meet the increasing demands and decrease production loses. IoT uses technology which integrates sensitive physical hardware with analytical software help in processing the data recorded by equipments / sensors. Most of the processing is done by machine learning and deep learning concept as an application of artificial intelligence where a computer / machine learns from the sensors data and makes future predictions. The performance of such a hybrid system mostly at the human level. IoT based smart hybrid system improves the entire system by monitoring the field in real-time. 

At the technical aspects, a real-time Iot data are produce, process and handle by machine only. It simplify machine learning and training aspects; while the data is with realtime accuracy, clean and less noisy. IoT data gives the flexibility, rapid model deployment to operationalize module building quality in less time. Iot with machine learning and deep learning technology used to project future trends, detect anomalies, and augment intelligence by ingesting image, video and audio also demystify the hidden patterns in IoT data by analyzing massive volumes of data using sophisticated algorithms. Iot with machine learning and deep learning make easy to identifies trends and patterns from the environmental data, the state of the environment and the impacts on ecosystems also handle multi-dimensional and multi-variety data automatically without any human intervention and shows the continuous improvement in the prediction. This technical intelligence perform cost-effective operation and gives better business opportunities.

// ALU Design in Verilog Exp 3

module alu(A,B,Op, alu_out);
input [3:0] A, B;
input [2:0] Op:
output reg [3:0] alu out;
always@(*)
begin
	case(Op)
		3'b000: alu_out= 0;
		3'b001: alu_out= A+B; // do add
		3'b010: alu_out- A-B; // do sub
		3'b011: alu out- A&B; // do mul
		3'b100: alu out- A|B: // do div
		3'b101: alu_out= ~A;
		3'b110: alu_out= ~B;
		3'b111: alu_out= 0;
		default: alu_out=0;
	endcase
end                          I
endmodule

//testbench

module TB():
// use same i/p and o/p as above
reg [3:0] A, B:
reg [2:0] Op;
wire [3:0] alu_out;

alu a1(A, B, 0p, alu_out); // call module by its name

initial
begin
	Op=3'b000; A=3'b0011; B=3'b0001; // 
	#10; // delay

	Op=3'b001; A=3'b0011; B=3'b0001;
	#10; // delay

	Op=3'b020; A=3'b0011; B=3'b0001;
	#10; // delay

	Op=3'b011; A=3'b0011; B=3'b0001;
	#10; // delay

	Op=3'b100; A=3'b0011; B=3'b0001;
	#10; // delay

end
endmodule

1. The proposed method employs the correlation matrix using Heatmap, required attributes are chosen, and the attributes that contribute more to the dependent variable are chosen. The six features such as pH,temperature, dissolved oxygen, ammonia , nitrate, and nitrite are selected.



2. The proposed system handles outliers and missing values through data cleaning. The proposed approach uses the substitute approach to retain the samples inside the dataset because the percentage of missing values in the dataset is slightly greater. The proposed system handles missing values by utilizing the k-nearest neighbour (KNN) imputer technique. The average of the five closest samples, present prior and next to the missing values, is calculated to replace the missing value. The number of outliers in a dataset is determined through data analysis. The outlier percentage inside the dataset is observed to be less than 0.15 %. As a result, the proposed method removes the dataset's outliers.

The forward feature selection (FFS) technique is used to find a subset of the best-performing features for the best model fit. If a dataset contains n attributes, the attributes are selected based on prior findings. After training each N feature, the proposed method evaluates the model's performance by employing the FFS method. After that, the best-performing feature (or set of features) is selected. This process is repeated until the proposed system obtains the necessary number of features. As a result, the FFS technique is a wrapper method for selecting the best subset of features. This process selects features like pH, TDS, DO,BOD, Ammonia-N,Conductivity, Sodium, Boron, Nitrate-N and Chloride.



3. The proposed system uses a modified isolation forest method to handle the outlier to reduce or control the increasing variability in the dataset. As a result, removing outliers leads the dataset to become statistically significant. 
Modified Isolation Forest

The isolation forest algorithm separates every point in the dataset and divides those data points into outliers. The division depends upon how long it takes to separate the points. The algorithm uses a non-parametric technique that works well on a large dataset with multiple dimensions(different features/attributes). The number of splits required to isolate a data point is known as the isolation number.  

1. Start
2. Select an attribute to handle outlier
3. Set the permissible range(minimum and maximum value)  for a given attribute/feature space.
4. Outlier <- search the data point for a given attribute/feature space  which do not follows the permissible range 
5. Find the nearest K neighbours data points that appear next and previous to the outlier.
6. Choose the Min and Max from K neighbours within the given permissible range.
7. Replace the outlier by random( min, max )
8. Repeat step 6 to step 7 until all the outliers' values are replaced.  
9. Stop the algorithm if there are no further outliers from all the attributes; otherwise, select new attributes and go to step 3. 
10. Stop

The proposed system uses a correlation heatmap matrix to select those features whose correlation is highest to the dependent variable.

Confusion Matrix is used to know the performance of a Machine learning classification. Confusion Matrix gives a comparison between Actual and predicted values. 

The confusion matrix is a N x N matrix, where N is the number of classes or outputs.

For 2 class ,we get 2 x 2 confusion matrix.

For 3 class ,we get 3 X 3 confusion matrix.

Confusion Matrix has 4 terms to understand True Positive(TP),False Positive(FP),True Negative(TN) and False Negative(FN).

Confusion Matrix allows us to measure Recall, Precision, Accuracy and AUC-ROC curve are the metrics to measure the performance of the model.



A confusion matrix is a tabular way of visualizing the performance of your prediction model. 




A Confusion Matrix is used to find the performance of a model and comparison between Actual and predicted values. To measure the performance of the model; the parameter such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, AUC-ROC curve, etc. are used. The Confusion Matrix make it easy to get the parameters at one place. The confusion matrix is a N x N matrix, where N is the number of classes used in multiclass classification. The four basic characteristics in confusion matrix are True Positive(TP), False Positive(FP), True Negative(TN) and False Negative(FN). It show's in a tabular way of visualizing the performance of the classification model.





the proposed method employs the spearman's rank correlation coefficient matrix with heatmap and measures the monotonic relation between independent and dependent variable. {fe_name} seem to have a monotonic relationship with dependent variable, as the corresponding values are greater than zero and closest to 1 and -1.

Adam includes the hyperparameters α (Stepsize / Learning rate), 𝛽1 (from Momentum), 𝛽2 (from RMSProp) as exponential decay rates for the moment estimates to compute f(θ), stochastic objective function with parameters θ. Initially, m = 0, the first moment vector, treated as in Momentum; v = 0, the second moment vector, treated as in RMSProp; t = 0, at time 0. At on the beginning of the iteration t, time increase by 1. Get the gradients / derivatives (g) with respect to t, here g is equivalent to (dw and db respectively). Then, update the biased as first moment estimate (m) with respect to t and update the second raw moment estimate (v) with respect to t. To keep the track of deflection in first moment estimate, compute the deflection of current (mt) and previous momentum estimate (mt-1) and stored in the deflection variable (mdiff). If the deflection variable is greater than the threshold value then compute the average of current (mt) and previous momentum estimate (mt-1) to introduce the smoothing / better estimate of momentum moving averages rate, else compute the bias correction on the first moment estimate (mt). Then, compute the bias correction on the second raw moment estimate (vt). Finally, update the weight parameters θ with respect to t and return the resulting parameters θt.

Aim : to improve parking facilities by the introduction of smart parking system that would reduce empty parking space searching time. 

Objective : 

These days, a few people buy cars even though they don't have anywhere to park them, and a few streets end up becoming parking spots, causing significant traffic congestion. Ordinary parking techniques are time-consuming and inefficient, especially in multi-story buildings where drivers must check each slot and walk through multiple floors only to locate and confirm a parking spot. The Proposed system (Smart Parking Systems) obtain information about available parking spaces by using image-processing. The proposed system utilize Python and the OpenCV library to detection of a parking spot is occupied or not from live video. Cameras were placed in strategic spots to detect ticketless parking. Authorized customers can get parking information from the realtime firebase database.

Proposed system : 

Users of the application may get information about every parking lot connected to the system. If there are vehicles recorded by cameras, the system will assign one parking spot to each car based on the number of cars that park there.


Proposed system architecture :

     <!-- Main Input For Receiving Query to our ML -->

      <input type="text" name="Frequency" placeholder="Frequency" required="required" /><br>
    	<input type="text" name="Angle of attack" placeholder="Angle of attack" required="required" /><br>
        <input type="text" name="Chord length" placeholder="Chord length" required="required" /><br>
        <input type="text" name="Free-stream velocity" placeholder="Free-stream velocity" required="required" /><br>
        <input type="text" name="Suction side" placeholder="Suction side" required="required" /><br>

    </form>

import pickle
from flask import Flask,request,app,jsonify,url_for,render_template
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

app=Flask(__name__)
model=pickle.load(open('model.pkl','rb'))

@app.route('/')
def home():
    #return 'Hello World'
    return render_template('home.html')

@app.route('/predict_api',methods=['POST'])
def predict_api():

    data=request.json['data']
    print(data)
    new_data=[list(data.values())]
    output=model.predict(new_data)[0]
    return jsonify(output)

@app.route('/predict',methods=['POST'])
def predict():

    data=[float(x) for x in request.form.values()]
    final_features = [np.array(data)]
    print(data)
    
    output=model.predict(final_features)[0]
    print(output)
    #output = round(prediction[0], 2)
    return render_template('home.html', prediction_text="Airfoil pressure is  {}".format(output))



if __name__=="__main__":
    app.run(debug=True)

1377x.to





The bias associated with the function is towards the subsets containing features that have high correlation with the class but are not correlated with each other [20]. It is used in the design of improved-RFC approach as it ignores irrelevant features as they have low correlation with the class. Redundant features are not considered in the resultant feature subset as they are highly correlated with one or other features.

decorators

# def fun1():
#     print('hello')

# fun2 = fun1
# del fun1
# fun2()

def deco(func1):
    def exefunc():
        print('runing exefunc')
        func1()
        print('done with exefunc')
    return exefunc

@deco
def myfunc():
    print('running myfunc')
    
# a = deco(myfunc)
# a()

myfunc()

param = {"asset_name":"KNC-PERP", "service_flag":"backtest", "cutting_logic":"gradient"}
response = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/call_one", json=param)
print(' -- > ', response.json(), type(response.json()))
print(f"-- > {response.json()['message']}")




@app.route('/api/call_one', methods = ['POST'])
def call_one():
    if(request.method == 'POST'):
        asset_name = request.json['asset_name']
        service_flag = request.json['service_flag']
        cutting_logic = request.json['cutting_logic']
        param = {"asset_name":asset_name} # KNC, GLMR, BTC, ETC, DOGE
        response = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/get_asset_data", json=param)
        # op : {'asset_data': data, 'message': msg, 'category': category, 'asset_name': asset_name}
        # print(' -- > ', response.json(), type(response.json()))
        # print(f"-- > {response.json()['message']}")
        if (response.json()['category'] == 'success'):
            response1 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/list_to_dataframe", json=response.json())
            # op : {'asset_data': df, 'message': msg, 'category': category, 'asset_name': asset_name}
            # print(response1.json()['asset_data']) # ['asset_data']
            # print(f"-- > {response1.json()['message']}")
            if (response1.json()['category'] == 'success'):
                # df = pd.read_json(response1.json()['asset_data'])
                # print(df.tail(2).to_string())
                param1 = response1.json() # param1 becomes type 'dict' from json format
                param1['service_flag'] = service_flag # 'live' or 'backtest'
                # print('response1 by kiran', param1)
                response2 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blip_and_entry", json=param1)
                # print('response2 : ', response2.json())
                # print(f"-- > {response2.json()}")
                param2 = response2.json() # param1 becomes type 'dict' from json format
                param2['cutting_logic'] = cutting_logic # 'delta' or 'gradient'
                param2['start_pos'] = 150
                response3 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cutting_logic", json=param2)
                # print('response3 : ', response3.json())
                dummy = response3.json()
                print(f'keys : {dummy.keys()}')
                return jsonify(dummy)





param = {"asset_name":"KNC-PERP"} # KNC, GLMR, BTC, ETC, DOGE
response = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/get_asset_data", json=param)
# op : {'asset_data': data, 'message': msg, 'category': category, 'asset_name': asset_name}
# print(' -- > ', response.json(), type(response.json()))
print(f"-- > {response.json()['message']}")
if (response.json()['category'] == 'success'):
    response1 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/list_to_dataframe", json=response.json())
    # op : {'asset_data': df, 'message': msg, 'category': category, 'asset_name': asset_name}
    # print(response1.json()['asset_data']) # ['asset_data']
    print(f"-- > {response1.json()['message']}")
    if (response1.json()['category'] == 'success'):
        # df = pd.read_json(response1.json()['asset_data'])
        # print(df.tail(2).to_string())
        param1 = response1.json() # param1 becomes type 'dict' from json format
        param1['service_flag'] = 'backtest' # 'live' or 'backtest'
        # print('response1 by kiran', param1)
        response2 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blip_and_entry", json=param1)
        # print('response2 : ', response2.json())
        print(f"-- > {response2.json()['message']}")
        param2 = response2.json() # param1 becomes type 'dict' from json format
        param2['cutting_logic'] = 'delta' # 'delta' or 'gradient'
        param2['start_pos'] = 150
        response3 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cutting_logic", json=param2)
        print('response3 : ', response3.json())
        dummy = response3.json()
        print(f'keys : {dummy.keys()}')




start_pos = request.json['start_pos']
        cutting_logic = request.json['cutting_logic']
        output = ml.FetchData.get_cutting_logic(asset_name, data, entry_date, entry_close, min_date, min_close, blip_date, blip_close, high_exit_date, high_exit_price, max_date, max_close, start_pos, cutting_logic)

dummy = request.json
        dummy['message'] = f'cutting_logic : {cutting_logic} is applied successfully.'
        if np.isnan(output):
            # ['asset_data', 'asset_name', 'blip_close', 'blip_date', 'entry_close', 'entry_date', 'high_exit_date', 'high_exit_price', 'max_PnL', 'max_close', 'max_date', 'message', 'min_close', 'min_date', 'service_flag', 'cutting_logic', 'start_pos']
            dummy['final_PnL'] = output
            dummy['cutting_status'] = 'No cutting'
            dummy['cutting_success'] = 0
            # print(f'output : {output} \n dummy : {dummy.keys()}')
        else:
            dummy['final_PnL'] = output
            dummy['cutting_status'] = 'Cutting done'
            dummy['cutting_success'] = 1
            # print(f'output : {output} \n dummy : {dummy.keys()}')
        return jsonify(dummy)

import random
def guess_me(birthdate):
    '''
    To guess my birthday (between 1-31) and
    the output from your program should be
    the number of trials or guesses your program
    made before you got it right.
    '''
    guess_birthdate, counter = 31//2, 0
    while True:
        if (guess_birthdate == birthdate):
            print(f'Your birthdate is {guess_birthdate} \nNumber of guess is : {counter+1}')
            break
        elif (guess_birthdate > birthdate):
            print(f'Number of guess is : {counter+1} || Guess is {guess_birthdate} .')
            guess_birthdate = random.randint(1, guess_birthdate)
            counter += 1
        elif (guess_birthdate < birthdate):
            print(f'Number of guess is : {counter+1} || Guess is {guess_birthdate} .')
            guess_birthdate = random.randint(guess_birthdate, 31)
            counter += 1
guess_me(22)





import random
def guess_me(birthdate):
    '''
    To guess my birthday (between 1-31) and
    the output from your program should be
    the number of trials or guesses your program
    made before you got it right.
    '''
    guess_birthdate, counter = 31//2, 0
    while True:
        if (guess_birthdate == birthdate):
            print(f'Your birthdate is {guess_birthdate} \nNumber of guess is : {counter+1}')
            break
        elif (guess_birthdate > birthdate):
            guess_birthdate = random.randint(1, guess_birthdate)
            counter += 1
        elif (guess_birthdate < birthdate):
            guess_birthdate = random.randint(guess_birthdate, 31)
            counter += 1
guess_me(22)

                    element.click()

        except: pass
        scroll_to_end(driver)





def save_valid_coins_data(self, tf, since='', limit=5000, low_market_coin=[]):
        self.infoLog('save_valid_coins_data method is called ...')
        self.valid_coins = self.get_valid_coins(low_market_coin) # get a list of valid coins
        if len(self.valid_coins) > 0:
            for c_name in self.valid_coins:
                try:
                    th = Thread(target=self.get_data, args=(c_name, tf, since, limit, self.exchange))
                    th.start()
                    self.infoLog(f'Current Thread is : {threading.active_count()}.')
                except Exception as e:
                    self.errorLog(f"Thread is not able to active for {c_name}.")
                    self.exceptionLog(f'Exception : {e} ')
        else:
            self.errorLog(f"List of valid coins is empty.")

obj.save_valid_coins_data('1m', low_market_coin=list_of_low_market_coin)

@app.route('/register', methods =['GET', 'POST'])
def register():
    msg = ''
    if request.method == 'POST' and 'username' in request.form and 'password' in request.form and 'email' in request.form :
        username = request.form['username']
        password = request.form['password']
        email = request.form['email']
        cursor = mysql.connection.cursor(MySQLdb.cursors.DictCursor)
        cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE username = % s', (username, ))
        account = cursor.fetchone()
        if account:
            msg = 'Account already exists !'
        else:
            cursor.execute('INSERT INTO accounts VALUES (NULL, % s, % s, % s)', (username, password, email, ))
            mysql.connection.commit()
            msg = 'You have successfully registered !'
    elif request.method == 'POST':
        msg = 'Please fill out the form !'
    return render_template('register.html', msg = msg)
@app.route('/login', methods =['GET', 'POST'])
def login():
    msg = ''
    if request.method == 'POST' and 'username' in request.form and 'password' in request.form:
        username = request.form['username']
        password = request.form['password']
        cursor = mysql.connection.cursor(MySQLdb.cursors.DictCursor)
        cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM accounts WHERE username = % s AND password = % s', (username, password, ))
        account = cursor.fetchone()
        if account:
            session['loggedin'] = True
            session['id'] = account['id']
            session['username'] = account['username']
            msg = 'Logged in successfully !'
            return render_template('index.html', msg = msg)
        else:
            msg = 'Incorrect username / password !'
    return render_template('login.html', msg = msg)






def get_profit_loss_consolation_status(self, df2):
        self.infoLog('get_profit_loss_consolation_status method is called ...')
        th_date, th_close, th_pct, th_max_pnl, th_pnl, th_time, th_status = [],[],[],[],[],[],[]
        for i in range(len(df2)):
            asset = df2['asset'][i]
            entry_date = df2['entry_date'][i]
            entry_close = df2['entry_close'][i]
            cutting_threshold = (df2['min_close'][i] + df2['max_close'][i]) / 2
            temp_df = self.get_temp_data(asset, entry_date)
            entry_index = temp_df.index[temp_df['date'] == entry_date].values[0]
            time_counter = 0
            for j in range(entry_index, len(temp_df)):
                if temp_df['close'][j] < cutting_threshold:
                    th_date.append(temp_df['date'][j])
                    th_close.append(temp_df['close'][j])
                    th_pct.append(((temp_df['close'][j] - entry_close) / entry_close) * 100)
                    th_max_pnl.append((((temp_df['close'][j] - entry_close) / entry_close)) * 150)
                    th_pnl.append((((temp_df['close'][j] - entry_close) / entry_close)) * 150)
                    th_time.append(time_counter)
                    th_status.append('LOSS')
                    break
                elif (((temp_df['close'][j] - entry_close) / entry_close) * 100) >= 4:
                    th_date.append(temp_df['date'][j])
                    th_close.append(temp_df['close'][j])
                    th_pct.append(((temp_df['close'][j] - entry_close) / entry_close) * 100)
                    th_max_pnl.append(150 * (df2['t_PnL'][i]/100))
                    th_pnl.append((((temp_df['close'][j] - entry_close) / entry_close)) * 150)
                    th_time.append(time_counter)
                    th_status.append('PROFIT')
                    break
                else:
                    time_counter += 1
            if (i+1) != len(th_date):
                th_date.append(np.nan)
                th_close.append(np.nan)
                th_pct.append(np.nan)
                th_max_pnl.append(0)
                th_pnl.append(0)
                th_time.append(np.nan)
                th_status.append('CONSOLATED')
        df2['th_date'] = th_date
        df2['th_close'] = th_close
        df2['th_pct'] = th_pct
        df2['th_max_pnl'] = th_max_pnl
        df2['th_pnl'] = th_pnl
        df2['th_time'] = th_time
        df2['th_status'] = th_status
        self.infoLog('get_profit_loss_consolation_status method is finished ...')
        return df2

@app.route('/api/call_one', methods = ['POST'])
def call_one():
    if(request.method == 'POST'):
        asset_name = request.json['asset_name']
        service_flag = request.json['service_flag']
        cutting_logic = request.json['cutting_logic']
        param = {"asset_name":asset_name} # KNC, GLMR, BTC, ETC, DOGE
        response = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/get_asset_data", json=param)
        # op : {'asset_data': data, 'message': msg, 'category': category, 'asset_name': asset_name}
        # print(' -- > ', response.json(), type(response.json()))
        # print(f"-- > {response.json()['message']}")
        if (response.json()['category'] == 'success'):
            response1 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/list_to_dataframe", json=response.json())
            # op : {'asset_data': df, 'message': msg, 'category': category, 'asset_name': asset_name}
            # print(response1.json()['asset_data']) # ['asset_data']
            # print(f"-- > {response1.json()['message']}")
            if (response1.json()['category'] == 'success'):
                # df = pd.read_json(response1.json()['asset_data'])
                # print(df.tail(2).to_string())
                param1 = response1.json() # param1 becomes type 'dict' from json format
                param1['service_flag'] = service_flag # 'live' or 'backtest'
                # print('response1 by kiran', param1)
                response2 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/blip_and_entry", json=param1)
                # print('response2 : ', response2.json())
                # print(f"-- > {response2.json()}")
                param2 = response2.json() # param1 becomes type 'dict' from json format
                param2['cutting_logic'] = cutting_logic # 'delta' or 'gradient'
                param2['start_pos'] = 150
                response3 = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/cutting_logic", json=param2)
                # print('response3 : ', response3.json())
                dummy = response3.json()
                print(f'keys : {dummy.keys()}')
                return jsonify(dummy)





param = {"asset_name":"KNC-PERP", "service_flag":"backtest", "cutting_logic":"gradient"}
response = requests.post("http://127.0.0.1:5000/api/call_one", json=param)
print(' -- > ', response.json(), type(response.json()))
print(f"-- > {response.json()['message']}")

import shutil, os
del_dir = r'C:\Users\Kiran\AppData\Local\Temp'
for f in os.listdir(del_dir):
        if os.path.isfile(del_dir+'\\'+f):
            try:
                os.remove(del_dir+'\\'+f)
            except:
                print('Not able to delete : ', del_dir+'\\'+f)
        elif os.path.isdir(del_dir+'\\'+f):
            try:
                shutil.rmtree(del_dir+'\\'+f, ignore_errors=True)
            except:
                print('Not able to delete : ', del_dir+'\\'+f)







Machine learning Life cycle

Gathering Data
Data preparation
Data Wrangling
Analyse Data
Train the model
Test the model
Deployment


Gathering Data : 

The initial stage of the machine learning life cycle is data gathering. This step's objective is to locate and collect all data-related difficulties.

The different data sources must be identified in this stage since data can be gathered from a variety of sources, including databases, logs, iot devices, the internet, and so on. The output's effectiveness of the model depends on the quantity and quality of the data collected. More data means a more precise prediction, thus more data is better.


This step includes the below tasks:

Identify various data sources : 
Collect data
Integrate the data obtained from different sources

Input: {Multimodal Data Modalities Di, D2, ..., Di....... Dm, Feature Sets F1, F2, ..., Fj,..., Fn and Classifiers C1, C2, ...., CK) ...... Cp}

Output: {Pfinal, i.e., predictions by the MMDD-Ensemble }

1. Preprocessing multimodal data D1, D2, D3, ....., Dm and Feature sets F1, F2, F3, ...., Fn 
2. Develop ML models C1, C2, C3, ...., Cp
TP+TN 
3. Monitor Acc = TPATNIEPEN for different D; under F; and Ck 
4. Discard D; with poor Acc 
5. Perform data partitioning: DTr = {DT, DI, DĮ........., DT and DT = {DT, D2, D3, ......., D]}, D??: Training dataset of the ith modality, D?: Testing dataset of the ith modality. o: optimal number of modalities. 
6. Fit base classifiers: m; = fit C(D?", Fi, Yi). mi: ith trained classifier, Yi: labels of the ith modality training samples. 
7. Pi = m;(D ) 
8. P] = m;(D) 
9. Evaluate final predictions:
Method 1: Voting
Pfinal = majority({P1, P1, PJ........., PT}) 
10. Method 2: Blending
CM = fit(P, Y) where Cm: Metaclassifier. P: Predictions of base classifiers on training data, Y: Training data labels.
Pfinal = CM(DT)



Input: {Training Dataset Dtr (X_train, y_train), Testing Dataset Dt (X_test, y_test), classifiers (C1, C2, C3, C4, C5, ..., Cn)}

Output: {MultiLabelModel_result, i.e., MultiLabel model files and Result file path}

1. Initializing with multiple classifiers C1, C2, C3, C4, C5, ..., Cn 
2. Develop machine learning models M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, ..., Mn
3. Monitor classification report {Accuracy, Loss, Zero_one_loss, Hamming_loss} CR1, CR2, CR3, CR4, CR5, ..., CRn on training Pitr = Mi(Dtr) and testing Pit = Mi(Dt) dataset for MultiLabel model
4. Save classification report CR1, CR2, CR3, CR4, CR5, ..., CRn and machine learning model M1, M2, M3, M4, M5, ..., Mn
5. Select top 3 model (M1, M2, M3) with best optimal accuracy score
6. Fit MultiLabel classifier model MLC = (M1, M2, M3)
7. Evaluate final predictions using voting method : Pfinal = (M1pred ^ M2pred ^ M3pred) / (M1pred v M2pred v M3pred)

⌄



We proposed an improved algorithm for MultiLabel classification problem. The proposed algorithm takes 3 input parameters, Training Dataset, Testing Dataset and a list of classifiers and  MultiLabel model result file with result file path. Initiating with a list of classifiers in step 1 after that multipal machine learning models are build in step 2. In step 3, classification report {Accuracy, Loss, Zero_one_loss, Hamming_loss} are generated on the basis of training and testing dataset and in step 4, classification report and machine learning models are saved. In step 5, the top 3 models are selected on the basis of best optimal accuracy score. In step 6, fit the model on optimal MultiLabel classifier and in step 7, evaluate final predictions using voting method.

def test_stationary(timeseries):
    rolmean=ts_sales.rolling(12).mean()
    rolstd=ts_sales.rolling(12).std()
    
    plt.figure(figsize=(18,10))
    plt.grid('both')
    plt.plot(timeseries)
    plt.plot(rolmean)
    plt.plot(rolstd)
    
    print("--------------------------")
    print('Results of Dickey-Fuller Test:')
    dftest = adfuller(ts_sales, autolag='AIC')
    dfoutput = pd.Series(dftest[0:4], index=['Test Statistic', 'p-value', '#lags Used', 'Number of Observations Used'])
    for key, value in dftest[4].items():
        dfoutput['Confidense Interval (%s)'%key] = value
    print(dfoutput)
    
    
    
    

import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
import numpy as np
import os
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from statsmodels.tsa.stattools import adfuller
import statsmodels.api as sm
from statsmodels.tsa.seasonal import seasonal_decompose
from statsmodels.tsa.arima_model import ARIMA
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error
import math
from pmdarima.arima import auto_arima
from statsmodels.graphics.tsaplots import plot_acf,plot_pacf
    






def test_stationary(timeseries):
    rolmean=ts_sales.rolling(12).mean()
    rolstd=ts_sales.rolling(12).std()
    
    plt.figure(figsize=(18,10))
    plt.grid('both')
    plt.plot(timeseries)
    plt.plot(rolmean)
    plt.plot(rolstd)
    
    print("--------------------------")
    print('Results of Dickey-Fuller Test:')
    dftest = adfuller(ts_sales, autolag='AIC')
    dfoutput = pd.Series(dftest[0:4], index=['Test Statistic', 'p-value', '#lags Used', 'Number of Observations Used'])
    for key, value in dftest[4].items():
        dfoutput['Confidense Interval (%s)'%key] = value
    print(dfoutput)
    
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

from statsmodels.tsa.arima_model import ARIMA
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error, mean_absolute_error
import math
from pmdarima.arima import auto_arima
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
def arima_model(X,y,arima_order):
    history=[i for i in X]
    prediction=list()
    for i in range(len(y)):
        model=ARIMA(data,order=())
        model_fit=model.fit()
        yhat=model_fit.forecast()[0]
        prediction.append(yhat)
        history.append(y[t])
    mse=np.sqrt(mean_squared_error(y,prediction))
    return mse

def evalute_arima_model(p_value,d_value,q_value):
    for i in p_value:
        for j in d_value:
            for k in q_value:
            order=(i,j,k)
            rmse=arima_model(dataset,test,order)
            
            try:
                if rmse<best_score:
                best_score,best_cfg=rmse,order
                print(rmse,order)
            except:
                continue
def evalute_arima_model(p_value,d_value,q_value):
    for i in p_value:
        for j in d_value:
            for k in q_value:
                order=(i,j,k)
                rmse=arima_model(dataset,test,order)
                try:
                    if rmse<best_score:
                    best_score,best_cfg=rmse,order
                    print(rmse,order)
                except:
                    continue

Collaborated with 2 data analysts and 1 project head to implement and analyze given data. Analyze, clean, and visualize different factors influencing the financial domain. class 0

Management 
1. Select Haste or Ambe bahar varities. 
2. Wider tree spacing, yearly pruning of trees. 
3. Proper disposal of diseased leaves and twigs.

Treatments or Pesticides
1. Carbendazim or Thiophanate methyl at 0.25ml/lit sprays. 
2. Kitazin 48% EC @ 0.20% or 80ml in 80 l of water as required depending upon crop stage and plant protection equipment used.


class 1

Management 
1. Wide row spacing. 
2. Selection of disease free seedlings for fresh planting. 
3. Pruning affected branches, fruits regularly and burning. 
4. Bahar should be done in Haste or Ambe Bahar. 
5. Give minimum four month rest after harvesting the fruits.


Treatments or Pesticides 
1. Before pruning it should be sprayed with 1% Bordeaux mixture. 
2. After Ethrel spraying Paste or smear with 0.5g Streptomycin Sulphate + 2.5g Copper oxy chloride + 200g red oxide per lit of water. 
3. Spray 0.5g Streptomycin Sulphate or Bacterinashak + 2.5g copper oxy chloride per liter of water. 
4. Next Day or another day spray with 1g ZnSo4 + 1g MgSo4 + 1g Boron + 1g CaSo4 Per litre of water.


class 2

Management 
1. Clean cultivation and aintenance of health and vigour of the tree should be followe. 
2. The fruits if screened with polythene or paper bags may escape infestation. 
3. Removal and destruction of all the affected fruits.


Treatments or Pesticides 
1. Spray Dimethoate 0.06% prior to flowering is important. 
2. In severe condition spray methyl oxy-demeton 0.05% and repeat after fruit set. 
3. Spraying of Melathion 1ml/lit.


class 3

Management
The plant is healthy.


Treatments or Pesticides 
The plant is healthy.


class 4

Management 
1. Affected fruits should be collected and destroyed. 
2. Select Haste or Ambe bahar. 
3. Wider plant spacing, yearly pruning of trees.


Treatments or Pesticides 
1. Spraying Mancozeb (0.25%) or Captaf (0.25%) effectively control the disease.


class 5

Management
1. Do not allow water to stagnate, try to create drainage facility. 
2. Do not irrigate for 2-3 days after drenching.


Treatments or Pesticides
1. At initial stage drench 2ml Propiconazole + 4ml Chloropyriphos per liter water solution, drench 8-10 lit of solution per tree. Drench with Formaldehyde @ 25 ml/l.

import sqlite3
import requests
 
conn = sqlite3.connect('BTC_Test.db')
c = conn.cursor()
 
def create_table(tname):
    c.execute(f'CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {tname}(Open_time REAL,Open TEXT, High TEXT,Low TEXT,Close TEXT,Volume TEXT, Close_time REAL,Quote_asset_volume TEXT,Number_of_trades REAL, Taker_buy_base_asset_volume TEXT,Taker_buy_quote_asset_volume TEXT,Ignore TEXT)')
 
def data_entry(tname,d0,d1,d2,d3,d4,d5,d6,d7,d8,d9,d10,d11):
    c.execute(f'INSERT INTO {tname} VALUES ({d0},{d1},{d2},{d3},{d4},{d5},{d6},{d7},{d8},{d9},{d10},{d11})')
    conn.commit()
    c.close()
    conn.close()
 
create_table('btcTest')
data = requests.get("https://api.binance.com/api/v3/klines?symbol=BTCUSDT&interval=1m&limit=1").json()[0]
data_entry('btcTest',data[0],data[1],data[2],data[3],data[4],data[5],data[6],data[7],data[8],data[9],data[10],data[11])
print('Done...')

# import sympy
from sympy import *
import random
 
data_x, data_y = [],[]
for i in range(101):
   
   
    rand_x = random.uniform(0, 0.099)
   
    x, y = symbols('x y')
    expr = x**2 + x + 0.99 # x**2 + 2 * y + y**3
#     print("Expression : {} ".format(expr))
 
    # Use sympy.Derivative() method
    expr_diff = Derivative(expr, x)  
 
#     print("Derivative of expression with respect to x : {}".format(expr_diff))  
#     print("Value of the derivative : {} ".format(expr_diff.doit()))
 
    ans_x = expr_diff.doit().subs({x:rand_x})
 
    # Use sympy.Derivative() method
#     expr_diff = Derivative(expr, y)  
 
#     print("Derivative of expression with respect to y : {}".format(expr_diff))  
#     print("Value of the derivative : {} ".format(expr_diff.doit()))
 
#     ans_y = expr_diff.doit().subs({y:i})
 
#     print(f'{i} : ({ans_x}, {ans_y})')
    print(f'{i} : {rand_x, float(ans_x)}')
    data_x.append(rand_x)
    data_y.append(float(ans_x))

Multi-procesing

import time
import multiprocessing
 
def calc_square(numbers):
    for n in numbers:
        time.sleep(2)
        print('square ' + str(n*n))
 
def calc_cube(numbers):
    for n in numbers:
        time.sleep(2)
        print('cube ' + str(n*n*n))
 
if __name__ == "__main__":
    arr = [2,3,8,9,12,23,34]
    p1 = multiprocessing.Process(target=calc_square, args=(arr,))
    p2 = multiprocessing.Process(target=calc_cube, args=(arr,))
 
    p1.start()
    p2.start()
 
    p1.join()
    p2.join()
 
    print("Done!")

# lib's
from flask import Flask, render_template, request
import time

# start app
app = Flask(__name__)

# routing
@app.route('/')
def index():        
    return render_template('index.html')

@app.route('/pipe', methods=["GET", "POST"])
def pipe():
    payload = {}
    headers = {}
    url = "https://link.json" 
    r = request.get(url, headers=headers, data ={})
    r = r.json()
    return {"res":r}


if __name__ == '__main__':
    app.run(debug=True) #, threaded=False

import time, winsound
import datetime

print('time start now : ',datetime.datetime.now())
start = time.time()
print('Stat : ',start,'\n')

while 1:
    time.sleep(60)
    print('testing at : ',datetime.datetime.now())
    end = time.time()
    print('End : ',end,'\n')
    # if round((end - start)/3600,2) > 1:
    if round((end - start)/60,2) > 1:
        for _ in range(1): winsound.Beep(frequency = 1500, duration = 900)
    
    print('Time to take : ',round((end - start)/60,2),'sec.')






# Check for cointegration
def stationarity(data, cutoff=0.05):
    if adfuller(data)[1] < cutoff:
        print('The series is stationary')
        print('p-value = ', adfuller(data)[1])
    else:
        print('The series is NOT stationary')
        print('p-value = ', adfuller(data)[1])

stationarity(ratio)

# Output:
# The series is stationary
# p-value =  0.018822874762815692

stationarity(ratio_1)

# Output:
# The series is NOT stationary
# p-value =  0.07090999553908879

import re
import requests

from datetime import datetime

link = 'https://www.instagram.com/accounts/login/'
login_url = 'https://www.instagram.com/accounts/login/ajax/'

time = int(datetime.now().timestamp())

payload = {
    'username': 'niteshkanojia8',
    'enc_password': f'#PWD_INSTAGRAM_BROWSER:0:{time}:Nitesh@12',
    'queryParams': {},
    'optIntoOneTap': 'false'
}

with requests.Session() as s:
    r = s.get(link)
    csrf = re.findall(r"csrf_token\":\"(.*?)\"",r.text)[0]
    r = s.post(login_url,data=payload,headers={
        "User-Agent": "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/77.0.3865.120 Safari/537.36",
        "X-Requested-With": "XMLHttpRequest",
        "Referer": "https://www.instagram.com/accounts/login/",
        "x-csrftoken":csrf
    })
    print(r.status_code)
    print(r.url)
    print(r.text)

    print(s.cookies)

Adam does not converge to an optimal solution in some areas. Adam can suffer a weight decay problem and also other algorithms like SGD generalizes better than Adam and thus results in improved final performance.

For certain instances, Adam does not reach the best outcome. Adam can have a weight decay issue, and alternative algorithms like SGD generalise more effectively than Adam, leading to better overall performance. To prevent that complex problems, we need complex solutions are, in each iteration to minimize the cost function with the gradient descent and moment estimator we calculate the difference between current and old momentum. If the difference is greater than threshold then we take the average of current and old momentum to optimize the new weight else used the standard way to optimize the weight.

Adaptive incremental learning is used to deal with unseen data. Comment on computational time complexity of retraining the model in adaptive incremental learning framework in case of real time data.


: Need of Novel Adaptive Incremental Learning Framework
A model can build a generalization capability based on a certain training distribution of data, and as its testing set is also from the same distribution, it ideally performs well. But in the production environment, when input comes from a very different distribution  that the model has never seen due to completely different environmental conditions, the model’s performance starts degrading. Overfitting issues occur when the statistical model fits perfectly against its training data. Whenever it happens, the model prediction gets wrong for new (unseen) data, defeating the purpose of the algorithm. This results in a problem referred to as “data leakage.” Therefore, machine learning models give poor accuracy on unseen data, and the testing accuracy of the trained model is affected.
To address this problem of unseen data, we have proposed an adaptive incremental learning framework. Data drift degrades the accuracy of the model.   Greedy method-based optimization approach is used for incremental learning to make the model adaptive. Data drift occur due to various reasons, such as calibration issues, sensitivity, and malfunctioning of IoT sensors. 

Model Deployment 
A. Client-side Deployment : It is essential to assess the input data produced by the IoT sensors before feeding it to the model. The proposed system performs the data drift analysis using rule-based logic, which is dependent on the data range. If data drift is observed, then such a record is neglected and intimated to the user for corrective action. If there is no data drift, then the input is acceptable and correct. This input is given to the trained model, and the output is obtained. The model's output is checked using output assessment and a feedback module by employing manual annotation or model drift Analysis. The predicted result may be wrong as the model's accuracy is not 100%. The output assessment and feedback module consist of two techniques, i.e., manual annotation and model drift analysis, which is an automated process to check the correctness of the predicted output. This study uses model drift analysis to assess the predicted output, and if it fails, a manual annotation technique is used.
Model drift calculates the similarity between the real-time input and the records presented in the dataset (D) using the Euclidean distance as a similarity metric. Then, highly similar N records from the dataset are used to compare the predicted output and actual output. If the similarity index between predicted output and actual output is greater than the threshold percentage P, then the predicted output is correct. Model drift analysis provides the similarity index percentage, and if  is less than the threshold percentage, then the manual annotation technique is used for input data validation. Manual annotation uses manual intervention to verify whether the predicted output is correct or not. If the output is wrong, then it is corrected, and the same is recorded in the new database. After executing the output assessment and feedback module, the input data is checked, and if it is found to be unseen in the dataset, then it is written into the new database. If the number of entries in the new database reaches K (Threshold), then a new database containing unseen data points is sent to the server-side module. 

Model Deployment at Server-Side
Whenever the data size reaches the K threshold on the client-side, the API pushes the new dataset from the client-side to the server-side module. The new dataset coming from the edge device is concatenated with the old dataset. The existing model is retrained using new samples. The proposed system keeps the weights of synapses unfreezed for the last layer of a neural network to have extended training. The model is asked to update the weights and make it retrainable based on new samples. The retrained model is validated for better accuracy to get the optimized model. The optimized model will replace the model on the client-side. The proposed system deploys the model at multiple sites on the edge devices (client-side deployment) to obtain a variety of data to enhance the model's adaptability.
